steps.causal_note: Metric calculations determined the structure of the deliverables.

raw_step.causal_note: Metric calculations determined the structure of the deliverables.
raw_step.observation: Calculated record ratios, pass counts, overages, and the latencyerror tradeoff score for each packet record. R1 had the best tradeoff score; R4 had the smallest total overage; all records failed at least one hard limit and therefore required review.
raw_step.output: R1 score 171.1797; R2 score 7998.5990; R3 score 1002.1835; R4 score 1092.9370. Baseline selected as R4; mitigated candidate selected as R1; improvement over baseline 84.33764251736376%.
raw_step.reasoning: A conservative baseline should reflect the closest-to-compliance fallback, while the mitigated case should capture the best packet tradeoff that still remains inside the packet-only analysis.
raw_step.state_change: Selected the conservative baseline and the mitigated recommendation, and fixed the controlling metric to decoder_latency_us * logical_error_rate.

task: Produce a launch-quality mini-benchmark comparing lightweight quantum error syndrome decoders for real-time feasibility using only the embedded packet. Build a reproducible script/workbook that scores accuracy-latency tradeoffs, preserves intermediate calculations, includes record-level citations and validation checks, constructs a decision table (baseline, mitigated, sensitivity), marks any contradictory or under-supported records as needs_review, and produces an expert-review memo plus final recommendation table. Recommended action must remain inside the provided budget and improve the controlling metric by >=10%. No external measurements, specialized licensed software, or hidden assumptions permitted; all calculations must use only the embedded packet.
